Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Adriana Ruano wins first Olympic gold for Guatemala

Adriana Ruano has made history for Guatemala by winning the country’s first Olympic gold medal in the women’s trap shooting event.

In an inspiring twist of fate, Adriana Ruano has delivered Guatemala its first-ever Olympic gold medal. The triumph comes after a career-altering spinal injury forced her to transition from gymnastics to shooting.

Ruano’s journey began with a dream of Olympic glory in gymnastics. However, while training for the 2011 world championships, she experienced severe back pain.

Scans revealed six damaged vertebrae, a setback that at just 16 years old seemed to signal the end of her gymnastics career, according toAP News.

“I was devastated,” Ruano said. “I had to face the reality that my gymnastics career was over.

But my doctor offered me a new path in shooting, and it became my second chance.”

Her transition wasn’t immediate. Ruano spent a year in recovery, adjusting to life with a back brace.

Her new path in shooting emerged as a lifeline, offering her a chance to remain in the sports world while safeguarding her back.

It was during a volunteering stint at the 2016 Rio Olympics that Ruano found renewed inspiration, according toABC News.

“Volunteering gave me a fresh perspective,” she recalled.

“Seeing the competition up close, I thought, ‘If I can’t compete in gymnastics, maybe shooting is my next opportunity.’”

That thought became a reality on Wednesday when Ruano clinched gold in the women’s trap event at the Paris 2024 Olympics.

Her performance was nothing short of extraordinary.

With an Olympic-record score of 45 out of 50, she outshot her competitors, including Italy’s Silvana Stanco and Australia’s Penny Smith.

Ruano’s final rounds were tense.

As she closed her eyes and took a deep breath before her 43rd target, it was clear she was focused on securing her place atop the podium.

Her subsequent two misses did little to diminish her achievement, as she had already amassed enough points to ensure her victory.

“This gold medal means everything to me,” Ruano said, visibly emotional.

“It’s the culmination of years of hard work, setbacks, and an unexpected journey.

I’m proud to bring this medal home to Guatemala.”

Her success is particularly poignant for Guatemala, according to theNew York Times.

Just a day before Ruano’s victory, Jean Pierre Brol won bronze in the men’s trap, marking Guatemala’s first medal since Erick Barrondo’s silver in race walking at the 2012 London Olympics.

Ruano’s gold represents a historic moment for her nation, underscoring the potential of resilience and adaptation.

In the women’s trap final, Stanco took silver with a score of 40, while Smith secured bronze.

The competition was fierce, but Ruano’s steady hand and strategic shooting led her to the top.

Ruano’s story is a testament to the power ofperseverance.

From a promising gymnast with dreams shattered by injury to an Olympic champion, her journey exemplifies the strength of the human spirit.

“I hope my story inspires others to find their own path, no matter how unexpected it may seem,” she said.

As Guatemala celebrates its first Olympic gold, Ruano stands as a symbol of hope and determination.

Her victory not only makes history but also offers a powerful narrative of overcoming adversity.

title: Adriana Ruano wins first Olympic gold for Guatemala
author: Precious Simpasa
description: Adriana Ruano has made history for Guatemala by winning the country’s first Olympic gold medal in the women’s trap shooting event.
